title: Bulgartransgaz Launches First Hydrogen Project with EU Funding
content: The European Commission has approved â‚¬4.56 million in funding from the Connecting Europe Facility for Bulgartransgaz EADâ€™s "Hydrogen Infrastructure in Bulgaria" project. This initiative, designated as a Project of Common Interest, marks the first phase of Bulgaria's hydrogen backbone and is the only hydrogen investment in Eastern Europe approved under this call. The project involves developing a 250 km hydrogen route connecting Sofia to the Greek border at Kulata, including two new compressor stations. It forms a critical part of the South-East European Hydrogen Corridor, which links Greece, Bulgaria, Romania, Hungary, Slovakia, the Czech Republic, and Germany. Executive Director Vladimir Malinov highlighted that this infrastructure will strengthen national and regional energy transition goals, positioning cleaner fuel alongside natural gas in the future energy mix. The funding will support feasibility studies, design, and environmental procedures.
author: Analysts Account
